mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which translates into higher corporate earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline, or 60-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of co-occurring growth months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns during tightening periods, or the predictive IC reverses sign when FEDFUNDS is rising.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation print exceeding 0.4% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX returns are positive in > 60% of high-inflation months, or the signal shows no predictive power in a walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market expansion, boosting consumer confidence and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative 60-day returns in > 50% of occurrences, or interaction term loses significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP reflects sustained aggregate demand expansion, which directly supports corporate earnings trajectories and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive months of declining industrial production signal a contraction in manufacturing activity and weakening economic momentum, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of double-dip INDPRO months, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark in out-of-sample periods.
